title: The Star-Lit Basket Adventure
summary: A mole named Disco finds himself in a most unusual home: a glowing basket perched atop a lion stool. Unlike his usual underground tunnel, this basket shines with a bright light that projects stars onto the walls and ceiling. Disco and his friends explore the dazzling interior before climbing onto the roof to bask in the starlight. Their quiet night is interrupted by the arrival of Popplemoll and his cat, Gemstone. Even the hooting owls join the group, as they all settle down together for a cozy story on their star-lit rooftop.

highlightQuote.text: And Popplemoll... and then Popplemoll said, 'Ding dong!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right, now it's time once again for Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So where shall we begin tonight, Izzy?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's interesting. What if you tried just this thing... like that? Oh, we get lots of tiny stars in one little ring.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't think that's a great idea.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No, okay. All right, there we go, stars all over like usual. That's nice.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's pretend this is a house. Mr. Mole, this is your house. Crawl through through through the tunnel and then you'll find the light inside. And you can do the mole things inside of it. Mr. Mole crawling through this window.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, we'll try it, says Disco. It's very different from the usual mole house. Normally we live in a tunnel underground and it's all cozy under the dirt. This is a basket on top of a lion stool with a light inside and it makes the whole room glow with stars. It's very bright inside there, but it is cozy. What do you think we could do in this kind of house? I don't think I could sleep in here 'cause it's kind of bright 'cause it has the light on.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Exploring!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, we could explore. What do you think we might find?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And if you go outside onto this part of the roof, you can sleep there. And then you can go back up. Mr. Mole, first you can go inside and explore. Mr. Mole, go inside.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, and so Disco and Disco went inside the basket. And they saw the very bright light and it hurt their eyes a little bit to look too closely at it, and so they looked away and covered their eyes for a minute. And they looked all around and they saw that there were little holes in the basket and the light was shining through the holes and making stars outside the basket on all the walls and on the ceiling. And they liked that very much. And they thought, 'Hmm, I wonder what it'd be like on the roof.' And so they went out the little door and climbed up the side of the basket and trumped up on top of the roof. And then they lay down there flat on their backs and stared up at the ceiling and it was just like staring up at the stars. And Disco said, 'Oh, there's a funny little shadow where some of the stars went out.' And Disco said, 'Yeah, that's us. We're blocking some of the holes so no light gets through.' And Disco said, 'Oh, that's okay.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco said, 'Do you like it up here? I wonder what we could do.' And Disco said, 'Well...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Exploring! You could go inside and explore, you can go on the roof and explore.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, we've been exploring. But now it's time to rest a little bit.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Go down, down, down and then go up, up, up.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco said, 'Disco, would you tell me a story? I'm so cozy and comfortable here lying on top of this basket looking up at the stars. Maybe we could tell some stories while we look at the stars.' And Disco said, 'Okay.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Popplemoll... and then Popplemoll said, 'Ding dong!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hi Popplemoll, what're you doing here?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want to tell a story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, we'd love to hear a story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, what do you want to tell it about?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Popplemoll, would you tell us a story about an adventure you had? Um, maybe it was a time that you climbed a tree, or that you met a bird, or went swimming?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um... you've got to make Popplemoll tell the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's my job. Okay, can you help me?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, so which one of those ones that I said do you want Popplemoll to tell about, or something else?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um... when he went down, when he went downstairs to check on his gems... to check on his cat named Gemstone. Wow, that's an interesting name. But Gemstone wasn't down there. He thought he was in the basement, and that was bright. And then he ate breakfast and checked on his cat again and played some games and then fell asleep and ate dinner the next morning and fell asleep again in this brush chair. That's what the story about.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco said, 'Wow, thanks for telling us that story, Popplemoll. I'd love to meet that cat Gemstone. What kind of a cat?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ding dong!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, who's there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's Gemstone, my cat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh my goodness, Gemstone's here. Hi.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hi.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did Gemstone say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Purr.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you must be a happy cat. Can I give you some pets? said Disco. Come on up here on top of the basket where we're staring at the stars. It's so cozy.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, but there's too much light for me.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Well, if you come up on the roof, there's not as much. It's less bright up here.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay. I'm moving up on top. But he still saw some... some owls by that light. So he hurried to Disco and said, 'The owls are making light I can't very see so much.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. We'll ask the owls not to do that next time.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And the owls say, 'Hoo hoo! Hoo hoo! Hoo hoo!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then the cat didn't run away. Instead, the cat curled up next to Popplemoll and Disco and Disco and they all lay there and had a cozy story time on top of the stars and stories basket. And that's the end of the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ah, that was a nice Stars and Stories time. Now let's go night night. Back on back.

characters.name: Disco
characters.description: An energetic Mischief Mole and superhero-clad detective, Disco is one of two identical twins who embark on grand adventures through the deep. Alongside her sister Thisco, this architect of the subterranean maps hidden realms—from frozen frontiers to the dusty canyons beneath the furniture—by piloting her signature submarine or racing toy cars through the bedroom's wild heights. Guided by "Cornebyborn" dances and Papa Mole’s lore, she finds solace within her glowing basket house, a dream sanctuary and fortress of a hundred locks. Whether navigating grimy domestic landscapes or open waters, Disco balances mechanical ingenuity with a persistent, soft-hearted wonder for the night sky.

characters.name: Popplemoll
characters.description: A mole character who tells a story about his search for his missing cat.

characters.name: Gemstone
characters.description: Popplemoll's cat who finds the starlight projections a bit too bright for his eyes.

characters.name: The Owls
characters.description: Nocturnal birds that hoot and contribute to the starlight atmosphere around the basket.

places.name: The Glowing Basket
places.description: A house perched on a lion stool that projects stars onto the walls and ceiling through small holes.

places.name: The Underground Tunnel
places.description: The traditional, cozy, and dirt-covered home where moles usually reside.

places.name: The Basement
places.description: This sun-drenched storage nook, home to Isabella’s weathered treehouse dollhouse and a collection of cherished older toys, remains notable as the place where Popplemoll once searched for his elusive cat, Gemstone. However, beneath the radiant dust motes, the space has become a secret sanctuary for mischief rats, who hide among the nostalgic relics to hoard and feast on scavenged trash.

places.name: The Roof
places.description: The top of the glowing basket where the characters gather to lie on their backs and watch the stars.
